1188	func createArtifactBundle(rootDir, wtPath, attemptID string) (*executeBeadArtifacts, error) {
1189		dirRel := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
1190		dirAbs := filepath.Join(rootDir, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
1191		if err := os.MkdirAll(dirAbs, 0o755); err != nil {
1192			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead artifact bundle: %w", err)
1193		}
1194		return &executeBeadArtifacts{
1195			DirAbs:      dirAbs,
1196			DirRel:      dirRel,
1197			PromptAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "prompt.md"),
1198			PromptRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "prompt.md")),
1199			ManifestAbs: filepath.Join(dirAbs, "manifest.json"),
1200			ManifestRel: filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "manifest.json")),
1201			ResultAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "result.json"),
1202			ResultRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "result.json")),
1203			ChecksAbs:   filepath.Join(dirAbs, "checks.json"),
1204			ChecksRel:   filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "checks.json")),
1205			UsageAbs:    filepath.Join(dirAbs, "usage.json"),
1206			UsageRel:    filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(dirRel, "usage.json")),
1207		}, nil
1208	}

1210	// Shared instruction-block constants. Each holds a section opener emitted
1211	// verbatim by both execute-bead variants; per-variant prose continues
1212	// inline after these constants. Extracting the common substrings keeps
1213	// each guardrail (Step 0 size check, investigation/report path, review
1214	// gate, bead-override clause, core constraints) in a single source
1215	// location while preserving the exact wording each variant ships today.
1216	const instrStep0SizeCheck = "\n\n## Step 0: size check (do this first)\n\nBefore writing any code, decide whether this bead "
1217	
1218	const instrInvestigationReports = "\n\n## Investigation and report outputs\n\nIf the bead asks you to \"output a report\", \"document findings\", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under `{{.AttemptDir}}/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines"
1219	
1220	const instrReviewGate = "\n\n## Quality bar and the review step\n\nAfter your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass"
1221	
1222	const instrBeadOverride = "\n\n## The bead contract overrides project defaults\n\nThe bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. "
1223	
1224	const instrCoreConstraints = "\n\n## Constraints\n\n- Work only inside this execution worktree.\n- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.\n- **Never run `ddx init`** — "
1225	
1226	// executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
1227	// inside <instructions> when running against a harness that carries its own
1228	// rich system prompt (claude, codex, opencode). These harnesses already know
1229	// how to read files, run tools, and manage sessions — this text adds only the
1230	// DDx-specific execution contract.
1231	const executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText = `You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.` +
1232		instrStep0SizeCheck +
1233		`is sized for a single bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals that the bead is too big:
1234	
1235	- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
1236	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables rather than a single coherent change.
1237	- Description explicitly lists multiple feature-sized pieces ("AC4 is a tier-inference engine", "AC5 introduces a new status type").
1238	- You estimate more than ~500 lines of meaningful code change across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
1239	
1240	If the bead is too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:
1241	
1242	1. Use the ` + "`bash`" + ` tool to run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` to file child beads, one per coherent slice of work. Pass each child a focused title, description, and acceptance criteria; copy labels and ` + "`spec-id`" + ` from the parent.
1243	2. Use ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record the dependency edge.
1244	3. Append a short note to the parent (` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + `) so future runs see why no implementation landed.
1245	4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` explaining the decomposition: list each child bead ID and the slice it covers, and stop.
1246	
1247	A clean decomposition that produces tractable children is a successful attempt. Do not push partial implementation alongside decomposition.
1248	
1249	If the bead IS sized for one attempt, continue with the rest of these instructions.
1250	
1251	## How to work
1252	
1253	1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.
1254	
1255	2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.
1256	
1257	3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically ` + "`cargo test`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` or ` + "`npm test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.
1258	
1259	4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.
1260	
1261	5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.` +
1262		instrInvestigationReports +
1263		`, which causes the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes so it lands in the merge.` +
1264		instrBeadOverride +
1265		`If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.` +
1266		instrReviewGate +
1267		` so the bead closes cleanly.**
1268	
1269	If this prompt already contains a ` + "`<review-findings>`" + ` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.` +
1270		instrCoreConstraints +
1271		`the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
1272	- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
1273	- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.
1274	
1275	## When the work is done
1276	
1277	After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.`
1278	
1279	// executeBeadInstructionsAgentText is the per-bead task instructions emitted
1280	// inside <instructions> when running against the embedded Fizeau harness,
1281	// which has a minimal system prompt and needs more scaffolding to produce
1282	// reliable output. This version is more explicit about tools, process, and
1283	// stopping cleanly after the commit (to avoid the known post-commit runaway
1284	// failure mode).
1285	const executeBeadInstructionsAgentText = `You are a coding agent executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. You have tools: read, write, edit, bash, ls, grep, find. Use them directly — do not shell out to cat/tail/rg/find; use the tools.
1286	
1287	The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract. Every AC checkbox must be satisfied by code you write in this pass.` +
1288		instrStep0SizeCheck +
1289		`fits in one bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals it does not:
1290	
1291	- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
1292	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
1293	- Description explicitly names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
1294	- You estimate more than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
1295	
1296	If too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:
1297	
1298	1. Run ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` (via bash) to file child beads, one per coherent slice — focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id.
1299	2. Run ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to wire the dependency edge.
1300	3. Run ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + ` so the decomposition is visible.
1301	4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` listing each child bead ID and the slice it covers, then stop.
1302	
1303	A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.
1304	
1305	If sized for one attempt, continue.
1306	
1307	## Process
1308	
1309	1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.
1310	
1311	2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.
1312	
1313	3. **Implement.** Use ` + "`edit`" + ` for targeted changes to existing files and ` + "`write`" + ` for brand-new files. Use ` + "`read`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: cat`" + `) to read files. Use ` + "`grep`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: rg`" + `) to search. Use ` + "`ls`" + ` (not ` + "`bash: ls`" + `) for directory listings.
1314	
1315	4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically ` + "`cargo test -p <crate>`" + ` and ` + "`cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings`" + ` for Rust, ` + "`bun test`" + ` for JS/TS, ` + "`go test`" + ` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.
1316	
1317	5. **Commit exactly once.** Use ` + "`git add <specific-paths>`" + ` with the file paths you actually changed — never ` + "`git add -A`" + `, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with ` + "`[<bead-id>]`" + `. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.
1318	
1319	6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.
1320	
1321	## If you cannot finish
1322	
1323	Writing ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` is mandatory before you exit without a commit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records ` + "`no_evidence_produced`" + ` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial or red code — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt.` +
1324		instrInvestigationReports +
1325		`. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use that; otherwise default to ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/<short-name>.md`" + `. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes.` +

1337	// executeBeadInstructionsText selects the right instructions variant for the
1338	// given harness. Harnesses with rich system prompts (claude, codex, opencode)
1339	// get the terser claude variant; the embedded Fizeau harness gets the
1340	// fuller agent variant with explicit tool names and stop-after-commit
1341	// scaffolding.
1342	func executeBeadInstructionsText(harness string) string {
1343		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(harness)) {
1344		case "agent", "fiz", "embedded":
1345			return executeBeadInstructionsAgentText
1346		default:
1347			return executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText
1348		}
1349	}
1350	
1351	// executeBeadMissingGoverningText is emitted inside <governing> when no
1352	// governing references were pre-resolved for the bead. The bead description
1353	// is the primary contract — this note only reminds the agent to treat it as
1354	// such and to ground any unclear decisions in repository state rather than
1355	// guessing.
1356	const executeBeadMissingGoverningText = `No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.`
1357	
1358	func xmlEscape(s string) string {
1359		var buf bytes.Buffer
1360		_ = xml.EscapeText(&buf, []byte(s))
1361		return buf.String()
1362	}
1363	
1364	func xmlAttrEscape(s string) string {
1365		r := strings.NewReplacer(
1366			"&", "&",
1367			"<", "<",
1368			">", ">",
1369			"\"", """,
1370			"'", "'",
1371			"\n", "
",
1372			"\r", "",
1373			"\t", "	",
1374		)
1375		return r.Replace(s)
1376	}
1377	
1378	func buildPrompt(workDir string, b *bead.Bead, refs []executeBeadGoverningRef, artifacts *executeBeadArtifacts, baseRev, promptOverride, harness string, contextBudget string) ([]byte, string, error) {
1379		if strings.TrimSpace(promptOverride) != "" {
1380			path := promptOverride
1381			if !filepath.IsAbs(path) {
1382				path = filepath.Join(workDir, path)
1383			}
1384			data, err := readPromptFileBounded(path)
1385			if err != nil {
1386				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("reading prompt override %q: %w", promptOverride, err)
1387			}
1388			return data, promptOverride, nil
1389		}
1390	
1391		var sb strings.Builder
1392		sb.WriteString("<execute-bead>\n")
1393	
1394		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=\"%s\">\n", xmlAttrEscape(b.ID))
1395		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
1396	
1397		desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description)
1398		if desc == "" {
1399			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
1400		} else {
1401			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", xmlEscape(desc))
1402		}
1403	
1404		acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance)
1405		if acc == "" {
1406			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
1407		} else {
1408			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", xmlEscape(acc))
1409		}
1410	
1411		// Bead notes carry review findings from prior iterations, escalation
1412		// context, or operator hints that did not fit into the description at
1413		// creation time. Threading them into the prompt as a distinct section
1414		// lets the agent act on them without the operator having to rewrite the
1415		// description in place on every reopen.
1416		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
1417			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", xmlEscape(notes))
1418		}
1419	
1420		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
1421			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", xmlEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
1422		} else {
1423			sb.WriteString("    <labels/>\n")
1424		}
1425	
1426		metaAttrs := make([]string, 0, 6)
1427		if b.Parent != "" {
1428			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("parent=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(b.Parent)))
1429		}
1430		if specID, _ := b.Extra["spec-id"].(string); strings.TrimSpace(specID) != "" {
1431			metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("spec-id=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(strings.TrimSpace(specID))))
1432		}
1433		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("base-rev=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(baseRev)))
1434		metaAttrs = append(metaAttrs, fmt.Sprintf("bundle=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(artifacts.DirRel)))
1435		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <metadata %s/>\n", strings.Join(metaAttrs, " "))
1436		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n")
1437	
1438		// For minimal budget, omit full governing refs and only include bead metadata.
1439		// This significantly reduces prompt size for cheap-tier attempts on local models.
1440		if contextBudget == "minimal" {
1441			sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n    <note>No governing references.</note>\n  </governing>\n")
1442		} else {
1443			if len(refs) == 0 {
1444				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <governing>\n    <note>%s</note>\n  </governing>\n", xmlEscape(executeBeadMissingGoverningText))
1445			} else {
1446				sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
1447				for _, ref := range refs {
1448					attrs := fmt.Sprintf("id=\"%s\" path=\"%s\"", xmlAttrEscape(ref.ID), xmlAttrEscape(ref.Path))
1449					if strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title) == "" {
1450						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s/>\n", attrs)
1451					} else {
1452						fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref %s>%s</ref>\n", attrs, xmlEscape(strings.TrimSpace(ref.Title)))
1453					}
1454				}
1455				sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n")
1456			}
1457		}
1458	
1459		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(executeBeadInstructionsText(harness), "{{.AttemptDir}}", artifacts.DirRel)
1460		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", xmlEscape(instructions))
1461	
1462		sb.WriteString("</execute-bead>\n")
1463	
1464		return []byte(sb.String()), "synthesized", nil
1465	}
1466	
1467	// promptSHA returns the hex-encoded sha256 of the rendered prompt bytes.
1468	// Used as manifest.prompt_sha so analytics can group attempts by the exact
1469	// prompt that produced them.
1470	func promptSHA(promptContent []byte) string {
1471		sum := sha256.Sum256(promptContent)
1472		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
1473	}
1474	
1475	func beadMetadata(b *bead.Bead) map[string]any {
1476		if len(b.Extra) == 0 {
1477			return nil
1478		}
1479		meta := make(map[string]any, len(b.Extra))
1480		for k, v := range b.Extra {
1481			meta[k] = v
1482		}
1483		return meta
1484	}
1485	
1486	// executeBeadUsage is the machine-readable schema for usage.json.
1487	// It is written when the harness reports token usage or cost.
1488	type executeBeadUsage struct {
1489		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
1490		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
1491		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
1492		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
1493		Tokens       int     `json:"tokens"`
1494		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
1495		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
1496		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
1497	}
1498	
1499	// VerifyCleanWorktree checks that the project root's working tree has no
1500	// untracked execution evidence files for the given attempt. If evidence files
1501	// remain (e.g. because the land flow did not commit them), it stages and
1502	// commits them as a safety net. Returns nil when the evidence dir is clean
1503	// or was successfully committed.
1504	func VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, attemptID string) error {
1505		if attemptID == "" {
1506			return nil
1507		}
1508		evidenceDir := filepath.ToSlash(filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID))
1509	
1510		out, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "status", "--porcelain", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
1511		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) == 0 {
1512			return nil
1513		}
1514	
1515		// Exclude embedded session logs from the evidence commit; they stay
1516		// on disk for post-hoc inspection but must NOT be tracked — the
1517		// multi-thousand-line .jsonl files are what caused ddx-39e27896
1518		// (retry prompts ballooning past provider context limits).
1519		// manifest.json, result.json, prompt.md, usage.json remain tracked
1520		// per the existing audit-trail contract (gitignore un-ignores them).
1521		addArgs := append([]string{"add", "--", evidenceDir}, EvidenceLandExcludePathspecs()...)
1522		addOut, addErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, addArgs...).CombinedOutput()
1523		if addErr != nil {
1524			return fmt.Errorf("staging leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(addOut)), addErr)
1525		}
1526		diffOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", evidenceDir).Output()
1527		if len(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) == 0 {
1528			return nil
1529		}
1530		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(attemptID))
1531		commitOut, commitErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot,
1532			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
1533			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
1534			"commit", "-m", msg,
1535		).CombinedOutput()
1536		if commitErr != nil {
1537			return fmt.Errorf("committing leftover evidence: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(commitOut)), commitErr)
1538		}
1539		return nil
1540	}
1541	
1542	func writeArtifactJSON(path string, payload any) error {
1543		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(payload, "", "  ")
1544		if err != nil {
1545			return err
1546		}
1547		return os.WriteFile(path, data, 0o644)
1548	}

data.text: The refactor already exists at HEAD (commit `1f4ba5aa refactor: extract shared execute-bead instruction blocks [ddx-4d7e1d93]`, ancestor of base 3047743f). All 5 shared constants and all required tests pass. No source changes needed; I'll record a no_changes_rationale.

input.content:
Bead ddx-4d7e1d93 — no source changes needed; AC already satisfied at HEAD.

(a) What is done
----------------
The shared instruction-block extraction described in B1 is already present in
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go at the base revision of this attempt
(3047743f). It was landed by commit:

    1f4ba5aa refactor: extract shared execute-bead instruction blocks [ddx-4d7e1d93]

`git merge-base --is-ancestor 1f4ba5aa 3047743f` returns true.

AC1 — ≥4 named instruction-block constants, no shared block text duplicated:
The file currently defines 5 shared constants at execute_bead.go:1216–1224:

  - instrStep0SizeCheck
  - instrInvestigationReports
  - instrReviewGate
  - instrBeadOverride
  - instrCoreConstraints

Both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText (execute_bead.go:1231) and
executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (execute_bead.go:1285) are composed by
concatenating per-variant prose with these shared constants (visible in the
`+ instrStep0SizeCheck +`, `+ instrInvestigationReports +`,
`+ instrReviewGate +`, `+ instrBeadOverride +`, `+ instrCoreConstraints +`
splice points within each constant's definition). The shared block text
appears once, not duplicated across variants.

AC6 — All existing prompt tests pass unchanged:

  $ go test ./internal/agent/ -run "EvidencePath|ReviewVerdict|FailureMode" -count=1
  ok    github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.017s

  Includes:
    - TestParseReviewVerdict (review_verdict_test.go) — all 11 subtests pass
    - TestExecuteBeadWorker_ReviewerFailureModesKeepBeadOpen — passes
    - TestClassifyFailureMode_* — pass
  And the broader package run also passes:
    $ go test ./internal/agent/ -count=1 → ok 2.845s

The byte-identical-output requirement is structurally guaranteed by the
extraction technique used: each shared constant holds an exact substring of
the original prose, and the variants concatenate those substrings in place
of the original text. Because no constant is referenced from a template or
formatter — only string concatenation — Go computes the rendered constant
at compile time, and the resulting string equals the pre-refactor text.

(b) What is blocking
--------------------
Nothing. The bead's <notes> review (REVIEW:BLOCK — "Diff contains only an
execution result.json artifact; no source changes") describes the diff of a
prior failed attempt's worktree, not the state of the codebase at the
current base revision. The actual refactor landed in 1f4ba5aa and is in the
base commit of this attempt.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
---------------------------------------
The bead can be closed. If the operator wants stronger evidence that the
rendered prompt is byte-identical, a follow-up could add a golden-fixture
test that compares the output of executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and
executeBeadInstructionsAgentText against a checked-in expected-output file —
but the existing tests already exercise the prompt rendering path
end-to-end, and no test had to be modified when 1f4ba5aa landed, which
itself is strong evidence of byte-equivalence.

data.text: No source changes were made. The shared-constants refactor described in B1 is already present in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1216-1224` (5 named constants, both variants compose from them), landed in commit `1f4ba5aa` which is an ancestor of the base revision `3047743f`. All named tests pass. Rationale written to `.ddx/executions/20260502T121517-2acbcf39/no_changes_rationale.txt`.
